title: Controversies over the role of government in the Gilded Age
description: Practice your skills in this exercise on controversies over the role of government in the Gilded Age.
topic_path: Controversies over the role of government in the Gilded Age | 6.12
Controversies over the role of government in the Gilded Age

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the question below. 

>“No industry will ever be given up, except in order to take up a better one; and if, under free trade, any of our industries should perish, it would only be because the removal of restrictions enabled some other industry to offer so much better rewards, that labor and capital would seek the latter. It is plain that, if a man does not know of any better way to earn his living than the one which he is in, he must remain in that, or *move to some other place.*” 

>-Source: William Graham Sumner, “Protectionism,” 1885

**Which of the following issues of the period was Sumner most likely concerned within the excerpt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: calls for government intervention in the economy to save failing businesses
labor unions organizing strikes to advocate for better working conditions
Congress developing new trade agreements with Asian countries
decreasing immigration from European and Asian countries

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the question below. 

>“No industry will ever be given up, except in order to take up a better one; and if, under free trade, any of our industries should perish, it would only be because the removal of restrictions enabled some other industry to offer so much better rewards, that labor and capital would seek the latter. It is plain that, if a man does not know of any better way to earn his living than the one which he is in, he must remain in that, or *move to some other place.*” 

>-Source: William Graham Sumner, “Protectionism,” 1885

**Which of the following developments from the late nineteenth century emerged from ideas most similar to those expressed in the excerpt?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the application of Darwin’s theory of evolution to explain the economic success of business leaders
the rise in public support for the idea that good Christians should fight for social justice for the poor
the surge in philanthropic donations by business leaders to support civic projects benefitting society
the increase in calls for the United States to restrict immigration and support domestic workers

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the question below. 

>“No industry will ever be given up, except in order to take up a better one; and if, under free trade, any of our industries should perish, it would only be because the removal of restrictions enabled some other industry to offer so much better rewards, that labor and capital would seek the latter. It is plain that, if a man does not know of any better way to earn his living than the one which he is in, he must remain in that, or *move to some other place.*” 

>-Source: William Graham Sumner, “Protectionism,” 1885

**Based on the excerpt, Sumner would be likely to support which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: business consolidation
antitrust regulations
minimum wage laws
protective tariffs

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the question below. 

> “Persons who possess the necessary qualifications obtain great rewards. They ought to do so. It is foolish to rail at them. Then, again, the ability to organize and conduct industrial, commercial, or financial enterprises is rare; the great captains of industry are as rare as great generals. . . . 

> “The aggregation of large fortunes is not at all a thing to be regretted. On the contrary, it is a necessary condition of many forms of social advance.” 

>-Source: William Graham Sumner, *What Social Classes Owe Each Other,* 1884

**Which of the following would be most likely to support the views expressed in the excerpt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a monopolist
a nativist
a Populist
a factory worker

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the question below. 

> “Persons who possess the necessary qualifications obtain great rewards. They ought to do so. It is foolish to rail at them. Then, again, the ability to organize and conduct industrial, commercial, or financial enterprises is rare; the great captains of industry are as rare as great generals. . . . 

> “The aggregation of large fortunes is not at all a thing to be regretted. On the contrary, it is a necessary condition of many forms of social advance.” 

>-Source: William Graham Sumner, *What Social Classes Owe Each Other,* 1884

**The ideas in the excerpt about the “aggregation of large fortunes” have the most in common with ideas associated with which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Social Darwinism
Social Gospel
Manifest Destiny
Isolationism

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the question below. 

> “Persons who possess the necessary qualifications obtain great rewards. They ought to do so. It is foolish to rail at them. Then, again, the ability to organize and conduct industrial, commercial, or financial enterprises is rare; the great captains of industry are as rare as great generals. . . . 

> “The aggregation of large fortunes is not at all a thing to be regretted. On the contrary, it is a necessary condition of many forms of social advance.” 

>-Source: William Graham Sumner, *What Social Classes Owe Each Other,* 1884

**The ideas about “the great captains of industry” expressed in the excerpt are most consistent with which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: laissez-faire policies
protectionist policies
interventionist policies
isolationist policies

assessment_items.question_content:
Examine the image and answer the question below. 

![Political cartoon showing Uncle Sam crumpling a paper labeled "Trade Treaty with China" over figures representing European nations. The caption reads: “Gentlemen, you may cut up this map as much as you like, but remember, I'm here to stay, and you can't divide Me up into spheres of influence.”](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/865caa2d73a4e7beeb3511feb0653fa3.jpg)

Political cartoon showing Uncle Sam crumpling a paper labeled "Trade Treaty with China" over figures representing European nations. The caption reads: “Gentlemen, you may cut up this map as much as you like, but remember, I'm here to stay, and you can't divide me up into spheres of influence.”

-Source: J.S. Pughe, “Putting His Foot Down,” 1899. Source: [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Putting_his_foot_down.jpg)

**The situation depicted in the image contributed most immediately to which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a policy that all nations could trade with China
a law limiting immigration from all Asian countries
a warning about European colonization in the Western Hemisphere
a global conflict between several European countries and the United States

assessment_items.question_content:
Examine the image and answer the question below. 

![Political cartoon showing Uncle Sam crumpling a paper labeled "Trade Treaty with China" over figures representing European nations. The caption reads: “Gentlemen, you may cut up this map as much as you like, but remember, I'm here to stay, and you can't divide me up into spheres of influence.”](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/865caa2d73a4e7beeb3511feb0653fa3.jpg)

Political cartoon showing Uncle Sam crumpling a paper labeled "Trade Treaty with China" over figures representing European nations. The caption reads: “Gentlemen, you may cut up this map as much as you like, but remember, I'm here to stay, and you can't divide Me up into spheres of influence.”

-J.S. Pughe, “Putting His Foot Down,” 1899. Source: [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Putting_his_foot_down.jpg)

**Which of the following changes to the United States during the 1890s most directly contributed to the development depicted in the image?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Foreign policymakers looked for trading opportunities with Asian countries.
Labor unions argued for limiting immigration to spur job growth in the United States.
Congress instituted tariffs to protect domestic industries and increase the cost of foreign goods.
Social reformers built settlement houses to help immigrants adopt US culture and the English language.

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the question below. 

>“Destiny and the vast future interests of the United States in the Pacific clearly indicate who, at no distant day, must be responsible for the government of these islands. Under a territorial government they could be as easily governed. 

>“The men qualified are here to carry on good government, provided they have the support of the Government of the United States. . . . If the American flag floats here at no distant day, the Asiatic tendencies can be arrested and controlled without retarding the material development of the islands, but surely advancing their prosperity by diversifying and expanding the industries, building roads and bridges . . . constituting a solid basis for American methods of government.”

>-Source: John L. Stevens, in a letter to John W. Foster, 1892

**Which of the following best represents continuity in the years before 1892 with the ideas that the author expressed in the excerpt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the acquisition of the Louisiana territory
the extension of voting rights to white men
the development of a national culture
the emergence of a temperance movement

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the question below. 

>“Destiny and the vast future interests of the United States in the Pacific clearly indicate who, at no distant day, must be responsible for the government of these islands. Under a territorial government they could be as easily governed. 

>“The men qualified are here to carry on good government, provided they have the support of the Government of the United States. . . . If the American flag floats here at no distant day, the Asiatic tendencies can be arrested and controlled without retarding the material development of the islands, but surely advancing their prosperity by diversifying and expanding the industries, building roads and bridges . . . constituting a solid basis for American methods of government.”

>-Source: John L. Stevens, in a letter to John W. Foster, 1892

**The ideas expressed in the excerpt contributed most directly to which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the annexation of Hawaii as a US territory
the creation of free trade agreements
the rise of political machines in cities
the belief in Social Darwinism

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the question below.

>“I think there are only three places that are of value enough to be taken, that are not continental. One is Hawaii and the others are Cuba and Porto Rico. Cuba and Porto Rico are not now imminent and will not be for a generation. Hawaii may come up for a decision at any unexpected hour and I hope we shall be prepared to decide it in the affirmative.”

>-Source: James G. Blaine, in a letter to President Benjamin Harrison, 1891

**Blaine’s remarks in the excerpt most directly reflected which of the following developments during the late nineteenth century?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the popular belief that the United States needed to expand to gain power
the emergence of anti-immigrant sentiment against migrants from Asia
the growth in voluntary organizations advocating for social reform
the rapid growth of cities due to falling crop yields on American farms

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the question below.

>“I think there are only three places that are of value enough to be taken, that are not continental. One is Hawaii and the others are Cuba and Porto Rico. Cuba and Porto Rico are not now imminent and will not be for a generation. Hawaii may come up for a decision at any unexpected hour and I hope we shall be prepared to decide it in the affirmative.”

>-Source: James G. Blaine, in a letter to President Benjamin Harrison, 1891

**A historian would most likely use this passage to illustrate which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: how foreign policymakers sought to gain greater influence in Latin America and the Pacific Rim
how the United States limited immigration to people from countries they deemed “desirable”
how Congress sought to limit the United States’ international presence and maintain isolationism
how the demands made by social reformers influenced American foreign policy decisions
